_meta.keyKnowledge: We work with music elements and compositional devices to communicate ideas when composing and performing, and we document our music.

_source.w: We work with music elements and compositional devices to communicate ideas when composing and performing, and we document our music.
y: Composing with purpose helps us express complex ideas and emotions through sound.
Using compositional devices like repetition, contrast and development gives our music structure and depth.
Documenting our compositions means they can be performed again and shared with others.
eg: We compose music collaboratively in response to an image, theme or question.
We improvise and experiment with sounds and digital tools to create moods and atmosphere.
We use notation — graphic, standard or digital — to record our compositions.
We compose a piece that uses contrast between sections to create interest.
We create an accompaniment using ostinato, drone or chord patterns.
We compose a soundscape that evokes a specific place or emotion.
